title: A lack of electricity and internet access hinders AI adoption in Sub-Saharan Africa
content:
Connectivity is no longer just about convenience, but also about fairness and fundamental rights.

Screenshot of the video, “Why there are so many power outages in Africa”, from the Afrique en 365 jours YouTube channel, created with Canva Pro.

title: India’s race to adopt AI sparks a deeper question: How can technology respect human rights?
content:
Technologies like AI-enabled surveillance and facial recognition are restricting civic space and aiding state authoritarianism

Originally published on Global Voices Advox

Image by Paula Villar for APC. Used with permission.

This article is part of the series “Don’t ask AI, ask a peer,” a collaboration among Global Voices, the Association for Progressive Communications, and GenderIT. The series aims to re-emphasise the importance of knowledge sharing among people, as has been done for decades. You can follow the series on

title: Africa has 2,000 languages. AI content moderation knows fewer than 20
content:

A smartphone showing a social media post in Ge'ez script with an automated warning overlay, symbolizing the challenges of moderating content in African writing systems. Photo by Pride Chamisa. Used with permission.

title: AI warfare triggers Putin, as Kremlin moves to dismantle the last pieces of Russian internet
content:
More and more restrictions are being imposed on the internet in Russia

Originally published on Global Voices Advox

Vladimir Putin does not use or know how to use Internet. Screenshot of YouTube video from YouTube Channel. Fair use.
